insight: In the 2026 FIFA World Cup, Saudi Arabia's performance during hydration breaks has been a mixed bag, with the team managing to gain momentum in only half of the breaks they've had, resulting in a slight decline in performance overall. When Saudi Arabia was pressing before a hydration break, they tended to lose momentum, with their performance declining at an average rate of 0.100 minutes, and they were unable to regain their footing after the break. On the other hand, when they weren't pressing before a break, they were able to maintain their level of performance, with no average decline in momentum. This suggests that Saudi Arabia may need to adjust their strategy during hydration breaks to better capitalize on their momentum and come out stronger after the pause.
